user_query:
I'm preparing a weekly maintenance note for my digital archiving prototype and need a quick, reproducible system status check. Please do the following using the local files in this workspace:

1) Run a validation command to verify that the file sizes in input/catalog.csv match the actual byte sizes of the listed files. Use the provided script and generate a machine-readable result:
   - Command: python3 tools/validate_catalog.py --catalog input/catalog.csv --out output/validation_results.json
   - The JSON should include totals (total_items, ok, mismatched, missing) and per-item details with id, file_path, expected_size, actual_size, and status.

2) Edit the existing draft at docs/weekly_status_draft.md in place to incorporate the results from output/validation_results.json. Specifically:
   - Replace the placeholders {{TOTAL}}, {{OK}}, {{MISMATCH}}, and {{MISSING}} with the corresponding counts from output/validation_results.json.
   - Replace {{MISMATCH_LIST}} with a bullet list of mismatched items in the form "- <id> (expected <expected_size> bytes, actual <actual_size> bytes)". If there are no mismatches, replace it with "- None".
   - Replace the line beginning with "TODO:" under Observations with a single sentence that starts with "Observation: " and states whether all files matched; if not, name the IDs of any mismatches.
   - Preserve all other content and headings.

3) Compose a concise status summary at output/system_status_summary.md that reports the four totals on separate lines exactly in this key: value format:
   - Total items: <number>
   - OK: <number>
   - Mismatched: <number>
   - Missing: <number>

Use output/validation_results.json as the sole source of truth for all numbers you insert. Do not hardcode values; they must reflect the current results of the validation command. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
